title: Bonney Sees Kuma’s Memories
description: On pages 1–3, this part focuses on Saturn, Unknown person #1, Kizaru, Unknown person #2, Unknown person #3, Bonney as Bonney discovers Kuma’s memories, revealing his Buccaneer heritage, Ginny, the Revolutionary Army, God Valley, and the painful origins behind Kuma’s sacrifice.
scene: Bonney discovers Kuma’s memories, revealing his Buccaneer heritage, Ginny, the Revolutionary Army, God Valley, and the painful origins behind Kuma’s sacrifice.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: One of the five elders, Saturn, has landed.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Rear admirals and captains stay behind while all other soldiers retreat from the center.

dialogue.speaker: Saturn
dialogue.text: The Five Elders has made landing

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: They aren't worthy of his presence.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: How did he even get down here?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I've only seen portraits of him.

dialogue.speaker: Kizaru
dialogue.text: What was that flash of lightning?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We usually call in an Admiral just one celestial dragon...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: "Are you telling me that man has come from Mariejois?"

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That's a world leader who reigns at the top!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: If I didn't want to get stabbed...

summary: This part advances the Kuma and Bonney’s Tragedy through 23 cleaned dialogue beats, focusing on character decisions, battlefield momentum, and the story’s larger consequences.
